action.hypothesis: JumpReLU can reduce FVU further at K=128 by making its gating sharper and more selective; increasing the initial threshold while narrowing the bandwidth should suppress weak activations more aggressively and improve reconstruction quality.
action.summary: Advance the `jumprelu` family from prototype to stabilization with a single family-specific parameter sweep point at the K=128 anchor. Keep architecture and optimizer fixed and test a sharper thresholded encoder to see whether the current 0.1888 full FVU can be pushed materially lower before considering any cost tradeoffs.
action.self_review: This is a focused follow-up on the only family with positive evidence so far. The main risk is over-sparsifying early and hurting reconstruction, but that is the right failure mode to test next because the current signal suggests threshold behavior is the key family-specific lever.
notes_to_memory: JumpReLU established the current K=128 full frontier at FVU 0.1887873373925686, so the next round should tune its threshold dynamics rather than reduce K.
Round 3 tests a sharper JumpReLU gate via higher init threshold and lower bandwidth as the first stabilization pass.
next_hypotheses: If the sharper JumpReLU setting improves proxy FVU, continue stabilizing within the family by tuning threshold strength before any K reduction.
If sharper gating hurts quality, try a softer stabilization point with moderate threshold and bandwidth rather than abandoning JumpReLU immediately.
If JumpReLU plateaus after a few threshold sweeps, introduce a new routing-focused family instead of continuing weak local search.
